canonical_definition: Across the provided literature, StableToolBench is consistently described as a benchmark for evaluating language models, with a focus on their ability to use tools. The most common framing is that it measures the "accuracy of function calls generated by models" ("Benchmarking Agentic Workflow Generation"), while other papers describe its purpose more broadly as evaluating "LLM tool invocation" ("Graphically Speaking: Unmasking Abuse in Social Media with Conversation Insights") or "tool use in LLM agents" ("MoRAgent: Parameter Efficient Agent Tuning with Mixture-of-Roles"). In practice, papers use StableToolBench to operationalize and measure the constructs of "Tool use" and "Generalization". Multiple sources note that the benchmark is derived from ToolBench and contains 765 solvable tasks or questions distributed across six subsets. These subsets are characterized as varying in "tool category and instruction complexity, ranging from single-tool to multi-tool instructions" ("Graphically Speaking: Unmasking Abuse in Social Media with Conversation Insights"). A frequently mentioned feature is its stability, which is attributed to a "stable API caching mechanism" or a "virtual API server" and "stable evaluation system". Researchers employ it in evaluations alongside other benchmarks, such as RestBench-TMDB, BFCL, GSM8K, and MATH, to compare the performance of different models.
papers: Graphically Speaking: Unmasking Abuse in Social Media with Conversation Insights
LLMs Trust Humans More, That’s a Problem! Unveiling and Mitigating the Authority Bias in Retrieval-Augmented Generation
ToolGen: Unified Tool Retrieval and Calling via Generation
MoRAgent: Parameter Efficient Agent Tuning with Mixture-of-Roles
Reducing Tool Hallucination via Reliability Alignment

definitions.text: A benchmark used to evaluate the accuracy of function calls generated by models.

relationships.paper: LLMs Trust Humans More, That’s a Problem! Unveiling and Mitigating the Authority Bias in Retrieval-Augmented Generation
relationships.evidence: We comprehensively validate the superiority of DTA-Llama in real-world tool benchmarks, evaluating its performance from three aspects: effectiveness, computational cost, and generalization ability. (Section 1)
